hook.text: Its name means handy human. Homo habilis had a bigger brain and smaller teeth — and made stone tools.

buildup.text: Homo habilis lived in East Africa around 2.4 to 1.4 million years ago, bridging australopithecines and later Homo.

discovery.text: With a brain about 50% larger than Lucy's, habilis could plan ahead and craft Oldowan tools with purpose.

twist.text: Some scientists debate whether habilis is truly Homo or a late australopithecine. The boundary is blurry.

climax.text: Habilis sits at the threshold — not quite ape, not yet fully human. A species caught mid-transformation.

punchline.text: The handy human opened the door to our lineage.

quiz.question: Why is Homo habilis called 'handy human'?
options: It was the first species associated with stone tools
